mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, labor market tightness forces wage growth that outpaces productivity, triggering inflation fears and Fed tightening expectations that drive negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a decline in the index signals weakening economic activity that reduces corporate profit forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in greater than 65% of observations when INDPRO declines, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during NBER-defined recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 3.0%, inflationary pressures erode real corporate margins and prompt aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while housing starts accelerates indicates a sectoral rotation toward residential investment, which supports financial sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of cases when the divergence occurs.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, a sharp rise in industrial production signals strong output growth that outpaces cost pressures, boosting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in greater than 60% of observations when INDPRO rises sharply during low inflation, or the mechanism fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.04 in out-of-sample validation.
